learning_outcomes.description: Explain the requirement of different protection schemes for the proper operation of power system.
Recognise factors to be considered when designing a power system protection scheme.
Demonstrate the operation of various components used in power system protection.
Calculate protection settings when designing a protection scheme for a simple power system network and components.
Identify challenges and current developments in power system protection.

assessments.due_date:
Assignment submission 1 20/08/2026 11:50 am

Assignment submission 2 8/10/2026 11:50 am

Due in the applied class. More information will be available through Blackboard.
assessments.task_description:
Two assignments need to be completed during the applied class in Week 4 and Week 10. More information on assignment will be available in Blackboard. All submissions are collected during the class. Overall worth of these submissions will be 20 marks. The assignments are equally weighted. 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person during the applied class. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
9/10/2026 5:00 pm

More information will be available through Blackboard.
assessments.task_description:
This is mainly based on PSCAD simulation exercise. PSCAD training will be provided for 5 weeks starting from week 3. More information about the simulation exercise will be available through Blackboard. A completed report based on the simulation exercise has to be submitted. More information related to the structure of the report will be provided through Blackboard. A viva is conducted during the presentations to discuss about your understanding about the content covered in the report and report marks will be weighted based on the student performance during viva. More information related to the marking criteria will be available through Blackboard.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
19/10/2026 - 26/10/2026

Viva will be conducted during week 12 and 13. More information will be provided on Blackboard. A time slot for each student will be allocated.
assessments.task_description:
This viva is to present the analysis performed through PSCAD. A viva is conducted in the lab enviornment and student will have the oopurtunity to present the work completed using PSCAD software. More information will be available in Blackboard including the marking rubric. Marks assigned for the report will be scaled based on the viva/discussion. The scaling factors will be availble through Blackboard in due course.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

learning_activities.topic:
Field visit

In this course it is planned that an excursion to a relevant factory or substation will be organised based on the industry availability. More information is available through Blackboard. Exact dates will be informed to the students well in advance once the industry confirms the dates. This may be scheduled outside of class hours.

Learning outcomes: L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Field visit In this course it is planned that an excursion to a relevant factory or substation will be organised based on the industry availability. More information is available through Blackboard. Exact dates will be informed to the students well in advance once the industry confirms the dates. This may be scheduled outside of class hours.

learning_activities.topic:
Lectures on various topics

Series of lectures covering fault analysis and various protection schemes used in the power systems. Some of the lectures will be covered by the industry experts. Following topics will be covered in this course : Fault analysis, symmetrical components, Relay operation, Different types of relays, Relay coordination, Unit protection, Instrument Transformers, IEC 61850 requirements, effect of renewables on protection schemes

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Lectures on various topics Series of lectures covering fault analysis and various protection schemes used in the power systems. Some of the lectures will be covered by the industry experts. Following topics will be covered in this course : Fault analysis, symmetrical components, Relay operation, Different types of relays, Relay coordination, Unit protection, Instrument Transformers, IEC 61850 requirements, effect of renewables on protection schemes

learning_activities.topic:
Applied Classes

Questions on fault calculations and various protection setting calculations will be discussed during these general contact hours. In week 4 and 10 in-class assessments are conducted.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Applied Classes Questions on fault calculations and various protection setting calculations will be discussed during these general contact hours. In week 4 and 10 in-class assessments are conducted.

learning_activities.topic:
ICT - PSCAD Simulation

Students will learn to use PSCAD software based simulations for checking relay settings for distance and over current protection schemes. Feedback for the PSCAD activities will be given during the in-class sessions. More information will be available through Blackboard.

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: ICT - PSCAD Simulation Students will learn to use PSCAD software based simulations for checking relay settings for distance and over current protection schemes. Feedback for the PSCAD activities will be given during the in-class sessions.  More information will be available through Blackboard.

learning_activities.topic:
PRA - Hands on experience on relay characteristic

Students will test relay characteristics on a Siemens distance protection relay Siprotec 7SA87. More information will be available through Blackboard.

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_title: PRA - Hands on experience on relay characteristic
